name: Rioja Alavesa Wine Route from Logroño
name_original: Entra la Rioja Alavesa y la Rioja Alta — circular por la ciudad de Logroño
description: This moderate 40.0-mile road cycling route from Logroño explores the vineyards and medieval villages of Rioja Alavesa and Rioja Alta.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Logroño traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive vineyards, rolling hills, and the Ebro River valley. The region offers varied terrain, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents into mountainous areas like the Sierra de Cebollera. Cyclists can explore well-paved country lanes that wind through historic villages and natural features. Best road cycling routes around Logroño The most popular road cycling route is Logroño: Viana, Moreda de Álava, and Oyón Loop, a 21.6 miles (34.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 34 minutes to complete. This easy route offers a pleasant ride through the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Rioja Alavesa Wine Route from Logroño, a moderate 40.0 miles (64.4 km) path. This route winds through the renowned Rioja Alavesa wine region, offering vineyard views. Local road cyclists also love the View of Trevijano – Soto en Cameros loop from Logroño, a 37.5 miles (60.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Logroño is defined by expansive vineyards, rolling hills, and the Ebro River, with options for various ability levels. The routes in Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 6000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Logroño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of road cycling routes can I find around Logroño?
A: Logroño offers a diverse range of road cycling routes, from easy loops through the countryside to more challenging rides with significant elevation gains. You'll find paths winding through extensive vineyards, along the Ebro River, and into mountainous areas like the Sierra de Cebollera. The region caters to various fitness levels, with well-paved country lanes and quiet roads.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Logroño?
A: Yes, Logroño has routes suitable for beginners and families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990957699/logrono-viana-moreda-de-alava-and-oyon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño: Viana, Moreda de Álava, and Oyón Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 21.6-mile (34.7 km) trail offering a pleasant ride through the surrounding countryside. Many routes along the Ebro River also provide gentler terrain.

Q: What are the best months for road cycling in Logroño?
A: The region around Logroño generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often ideal, with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery as the vineyards change color. Summers can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still enjoyable. Winters are generally mild, but it's advisable to check local weather conditions.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through the famous Rioja wine region?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are specifically designed to showcase the renowned Rioja Alavesa and Rioja Alta wine regions. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990734369/rioja-alavesa-wine-route-from-logrono" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioja Alavesa Wine Route from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 40-mile (64.4 km) path that winds through endless vineyards and offers opportunities to visit historic wineries.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For cyclists seeking a challenge, Logroño offers routes with substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Trevijano – Soto en Cameros loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example, featuring over 1,000 meters of elevation gain across its 37.5 miles (60.4 km), leading through varied and mountainous landscapes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Logroño's road cycling routes are rich with sights. You can cycle past historic medieval villages like Laguardia and Santo Domingo de la Calzada, explore the vast vineyards of Rioja, or follow sections of the Camino de Santiago. Natural features include the Ebro River, the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the distant Sierra de Cebollera Natural Park with its forests and waterfalls. You might also spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4509944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osborne Bull&lt;/a&gt; or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of the Witch's Hut&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Logroño?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Logroño are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990957699/logrono-viana-moreda-de-alava-and-oyon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño: Viana, Moreda de Álava, and Oyón Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Trevijano – Soto en Cameros loop from Logroño&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploring the region.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Logroño?
A: The road cycling routes in Logroño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning vineyard landscapes, the quiet country lanes, and the variety of terrain that caters to all skill levels, from gentle riverside paths to challenging climbs.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that follow the Ebro River?
A: Yes, cycling alongside the Ebro River is a popular option in Logroño. The riverbanks offer picturesque paths, and you can easily join these routes from the city, often via landmarks like the Puente de Hierro or Puente de Piedra. These routes provide a different kind of experience, often with well-conditioned paths suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Logroño?
A: Logroño offers various parking options, particularly around the city center and near popular access points for routes. Many routes start directly from Logroño, making it convenient to park in urban areas or designated parking lots. For routes venturing further out, look for parking in the smaller villages you plan to start from, or near specific attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I integrate public transport with my road cycling trips in Logroño?
A: While specific public transport options for bikes can vary, Logroño is well-connected by regional buses and trains. It's advisable to check with local transport providers regarding their policies for carrying bicycles, especially for longer journeys or during peak times. Within the city, cycling is a popular way to get around, and many routes are easily accessible from urban areas.

Q: Are there opportunities to stop for food or accommodation along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is well-equipped with amenities for cyclists. Many routes pass through charming medieval towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and local bodegas for refreshments. Logroño itself offers a wide range of accommodation and dining options. For routes through the wine regions, you'll often find opportunities for wine tastings and tours.
tips: This is a great place to start or end an adventure. From here you have views of all the surroundings below. There is a water fountain and there is a parking very close.

El Ciego is an exemplary municipality in the province of Logroño as it is surrounded by cultivated vineyards and a set of wine cellars. Surrounding it are the fields of vineyards that offer a landscape archetype typical of this area.
A good place to refuel, an obligatory stop on the Camino de Santiago. A town with an important pottery and wine tradition.

Logroño is the capital of the province of La Rioja and located on the banks of the Ebro River that flows into the Mediterranean Sea. With a famous square and cathedral, it is a center with more than 150,000 inhabitants.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. It covers 64.4 kilometers with an elevation gain of approximately 740 meters. A good level of fitness and familiarity with riding longer distances is recommended.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route that starts and ends in the city of Logroño, taking you through the Rioja Alavesa and Rioja Alta regions.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route in Logroño?
faqs.answer: As the route is circular through Logroño, you can find various public parking options within the city. Consider parking near the city center or on its outskirts, depending on your preferred starting point.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily uses smooth, paved bike paths and quiet side roads. While generally well-maintained, you may encounter occasional short stretches on busier roads. There are moderate climbs to hill towns and undulating sections, contributing to the route's moderate difficulty.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking scenery dominated by the iconic vineyards of La Rioja, often described as a 'sea of infinite vineyards.' The route also offers views of the Cantabrian mountains to the north and the Sierras de Cameros and La Demanda to the south. You'll pass through charming medieval villages and historical sites.

faqs.question: What are some notable places or highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several interesting locations. You'll cycle near the historic town of Laguardia and the settlement of El Ciego. You might also spot the Fountain in Navarrete and, of course, the vibrant city of Logroño itself. Keep an eye out for modern wineries with avant-garde designs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle in the Rioja region is typically during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when the weather is pleasant, and the vineyards offer beautiful colors. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, it's not typically suitable for cycling with dogs unless they are in a specialized trailer. Always ensure your dog is safe and comfortable, and check local regulations for specific towns you pass through.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths through the Rioja Alavesa and Rioja Alta regions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route briefly overlap with other paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camino natural de Ebro - Tramo central, the Ignatian Way, and the Camino de Santiago, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, bring your racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles, snacks, a repair kit (spare tube, pump, levers), and sun protection. Given the wine region, consider a small backpack for any souvenirs or purchases from local wineries.
